categories.title: Briefings
categories.description: Executive briefings, governance frameworks, and agency overviews for California state government leaders and stakeholders.

categories.title: Training
categories.description: Structured learning paths for state employees. Complete modules in order to build practical AI and technology skills.

categories.title: FellowOps
categories.description: Operational resources for Cohort 1 Senior Fellows. Meeting cadence, deliverables, and coordination.

Building a Presentation Pipeline with AI
WaterBot: Building a Gov AI Assistant

briefings.title: California Enterprise Modernization Plan
briefings.description: Governor's Office briefing on the comprehensive state IT modernization strategy. Covers E3 accountability model, Technical Executive Assignment framework, data governance, $100M Breakthrough Fund, and procurement reform.

briefings.title: Technical Executive Standards Initiative
briefings.description: Cabinet briefing on the TEA framework. Establishes verified competence requirements for CIO, CISO, CDO, and CTO positions in California state government.

briefings.title: Claude Code Deployment at SWRCB
briefings.description: Executive briefing for deploying AI coding assistants in California state government. Covers compliance requirements, risk classification, technical controls, and phased implementation.

briefings.title: Digital Transformation in Corrections
briefings.description: Strategic framework for modernizing correctional facility operations through secure technology adoption, AI-assisted case management, and data-driven decision making.

briefings.title: CDCR Technology Landscape
briefings.description: Current state of technology at CDCR. Maps existing systems, infrastructure challenges, and modernization opportunities across California's correctional institutions.

training.courseDescription: A 5-module course designed for California state employees with 10+ years of experience and spreadsheet-level technical skills. Learn to use AI tools effectively, responsibly, and in compliance with state policy.

modules.title: Perplexity AI for Government
modules.description: Sign up for free Pro access with your .gov email, learn the interface, write effective prompts, use Deep Research, and manage privacy settings.

modules.title: GitHub for Non-Coders
modules.description: Why 362+ government orgs use GitHub for documents, policies, and collaboration — not just code. Learn Markdown, the web UI, pull request reviews, and version control.

modules.title: Search, Research & Verification with AI
modules.description: Using AI tools for research while maintaining accuracy and trust.

modules.title: Prompt Engineering for Government Work
modules.description: Writing effective prompts for policy analysis, drafting, and decision support.

modules.title: Quality Assurance for RAG Knowledge Bases
modules.description: Best practices for ensuring data quality in RAG systems: deduplication, validation, embedding integrity, and testing.

modules.title: The Agentic Future: AI in Government Operations
modules.description: Where AI is headed and what it means for California state services.

modules.title: WaterBot: Building a Government AI Assistant
modules.description: Case study of building a production RAG chatbot for California Water Boards. Covers architecture, knowledge curation, semantic chunking, adversarial QA testing, and a replicable blueprint.

modules.title: Building a Presentation Pipeline with AI
modules.description: How a Claude Code custom skill turns markdown into standalone Reveal.js HTML presentations. Covers the 3-stage pipeline, component library, design system, and a reproducible MVP recipe.

modules.title: Production Hardening for Government Web Services
modules.description: Real metrics from a 6-phase hardening pipeline: rate limiting, memory caps, Redis caching, Cloudflare edge protection, and k6 load testing — all at $0 cost. 50+ verified before/after metrics.
